dataset.name: EID Quick Reference - Windows OpenSSH Server

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.notice: Event descriptions are paraphrased summaries written for this dataset. Source links point to authoritative references.

entries.title: SSH authentication event
entries.summary: The Windows OpenSSH server processed an authentication or session lifecycle event — accepted, failed, or session open/close.
entries.details: Logged by the OpenSSH SSH Server provider in the Application log for all SSH authentication and session lifecycle events. All sub-types share EID 4; the specific event is identified by the Message field content. Sub-types include 'Accepted publickey', 'Accepted password', 'Failed password', 'Failed publickey', 'session opened for user <user>', and 'session closed for user <user>'. An 'Invalid user <username>' prefix on a failed event indicates the username does not exist on the system.

prerequisites.description: Windows OpenSSH Server must be installed as an optional feature (not installed by default). Install via Settings > Optional Features or PowerShell.
OpenSSH logging level must be set to INFO or higher in sshd_config (default is INFO). Ensure the sshd service is started and set to automatic.

investigationPivots: Source IP not matching known admin or jump host IP ranges on an 'Accepted' event is the primary lateral movement pivot
Accepted password auth on a server where PasswordAuthentication should be disabled indicates either misconfiguration or attacker re-enablement of password auth
Unexpected public key fingerprint in 'Accepted publickey' — cross-reference against authorized_keys files for each user; a new or unrecognised fingerprint indicates persistence via added SSH key
High volume of 'Failed password' or 'Invalid user' from a single IP indicates brute force; multiple source IPs with low per-IP volume indicates credential spray
Very short session duration (session opened immediately followed by closed within seconds) from an unusual source indicates scripted C2 or automated data staging
SSH access to domain controllers or Tier 0 infrastructure from any non-privileged workstation is always anomalous
commonFalsePositives: Automated deployment tools (Ansible, Puppet, Chef) performing SSH-based configuration management
Administrators using SSH as a preferred remote management method on servers where it is authorised
SFTP transfers from backup and monitoring systems
CI/CD pipeline runners connecting via SSH to deploy applications

keyFields.name: Message
keyFields.description: Full sshd message text; parse to extract sub-type (Accepted/Failed/session), username, source IP, port, and auth method (publickey/password)

detectionRules.title: SSH Brute Force — High Volume Failed Authentications
detectionRules.rule:
Event
| where Source == "OpenSSH SSH Server"
| where EventID == 4
| where RenderedDescription has "Failed"
| parse RenderedDescription with * "from " SourceIP " port" *
| summarize FailureCount = count() by SourceIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where FailureCount > 20
| project TimeGenerated, SourceIP, FailureCount
detectionRules.notes: Tune the threshold to your environment. On internet-facing SSH servers, consider a lower threshold (10+ in 5 minutes). Combine with geo-IP enrichment for higher fidelity.

detectionRules.title: SSH Password Authentication Accepted on Hardened Host
detectionRules.rule:
Event
| where Source == "OpenSSH SSH Server"
| where EventID == 4
| where RenderedDescription has "Accepted password"
| parse RenderedDescription with * "for " Username " from " SourceIP " port" *
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Username, SourceIP, RenderedDescription
detectionRules.notes: Password auth should be disabled (PasswordAuthentication no) on hardened servers. Any accepted password login warrants review.

detectionRules.title: SSH Authentication Accepted via Password on Windows Host
detectionRules.rule:
title: SSH Authentication Accepted via Password on Windows Host
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: application
    definition: Source must be 'OpenSSH SSH Server'
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 4
        Message|contains: 'Accepted password'
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Hosts where password authentication is intentionally permitted
    - Legacy automation that cannot use key-based auth
level: medium
